rulings.Case Title: HARTFORD ACCIDENT & INDEMNITY COMPANY VS. LISA NASH et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 3, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANT LISA NASH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. The unpublished decision of the Court of Appeals in the related case of Harford Accident & Indemnity Co. v. Pacific Gas & Electric Co. (2008) A119344, Superior Court # 453038 addresses the central legal and factual issues raised by this motion. The Court of Appeals held that Hartford has a viable claim against PG&E to recoup workers' compensation benefits paid to Ms. Nash. In so ruling, the Court cited the settlement agreement between PG&E and the Nash family wherein Ms. Nash is obligated to "defend any complaint brought to recover such liens or benefits, and pay such liens or benefits from the settlement proceeds." (Footnote 3.) As a consequence, Nash must defend the Hartford-PG&E action upon remand but that does not provide a basis to proceed directly against Nash. The Nash-PG&E agreement is a part of the record in this case. The facts cited by the Court of Appeals are also the undisputed facts in this case - when the Nash suit was filed against PG&E, when the settlement was reached and notice to Harford prior to the dismissal of the action. The record remains undisputed that Harford did not file a lien or intervene in the underlying case after receiving notice. The question presented is whether Hartford may maintain this direct action against Nash. Absent the filing of a lien in the Nash-PG&E action, there does not appear to be a basis for Harford to bring this action against Nash. See Marrujo v. Hunt (1977) 71 Cal.App.3d 972, 977-978; Labor Code section 3856(b) and the Court of Appeals decision in Hartford v. PG&E, page 1. It appears that any direct action, absent a lien, must be brought in the workers' compensation system. Perrillo v. Picco & Presley (2007) 157 Cal.App.4th 914, n.3. This does not mean that Harford is without a remedy or that Nash will receive a windfall. What it means is that Harford pursued the correct remedy when it proceeded in
